subject: [PATCH v3] x86/virt/tdx: Formalize SEAMCALL version encoding support

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau
reviews.summary: Expressed neutrality on the patch's approach, stating it is not a hill to die on but still results in centralized and correct encoding. Provided Reviewed-by tag.

2026-07-22.patch_summary: The patch formalizes SEAMCALL version encoding support in x86/virt/tdx.

reviews.author: Xu Yilun
reviews.summary: Acknowledged Kiryl's point, stating that the patch provides a consistent view of what 'fn' is for all seamcall-related functions.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, Jul 22, 2026 at 12:23:04PM +0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:
> On Wed, Jul 22, 2026 at 04:46:34PM +0800, Xu Yilun wrote:
> > While both options have merits, I chose the latter from a
> > software-centric perspective. I sent v3 to invite more discussion.
> 
> I still don't see what postponing the encoding to assembly buys us,

Sorry, I overlooked it. I think this gives us consistent view of what
'fn' is for all seamcall-related functions.

> but it is not the hill I want to die on. The result is centralized
> and correct, so let's move on.
> 
> Reviewed-by: Kiryl Shutsemau (Meta) <kas@kernel.org>

Thanks!

2026-07-23.patch_summary: The patch formalizes SEAMCALL version encoding support in x86/virt/tdx.
